2603.25203.title: Probabilistic Concept Graph Reasoning for Multimodal Misinformation Detection
2603.25203.paper_summary: Multimodal misinformation poses an escalating challenge that often evades traditional detectors, which are opaque black boxes and fragile against new manipulation tactics. We present Probabilistic Concept Graph Reasoning (PCGR), an interpretable and evolvable framework that reframes multimodal misinformation detection (MMD) as structured and concept-based reasoning. PCGR follows a build-then-infer paradigm, which first constructs a graph of human-understandable concept nodes, including novel high-level concepts automatically discovered and validated by multimodal large language models (MLLMs), and then applies hierarchical attention over this concept graph to infer claim veracity. This design produces interpretable reasoning chains linking evidence to conclusions. Experiments demonstrate that PCGR achieves state-of-the-art MMD accuracy and robustness to emerging manipulation types, outperforming prior methods in both coarse detection and fine-grained manipulation recognition.
2603.25203.paper_summary_zh: 多模態錯誤資訊帶來了日益嚴峻的挑戰，這種挑戰常常逃避傳統檢測器的監測，這些檢測器是黑箱且對新的操控策略脆弱。我們提出了概率概念圖推理（PCGR），這是一個可解釋且可演變的框架，將多模態錯誤資訊檢測（MMD）重新定義為結構化和基於概念的推理。PCGR遵循先構建再推理的範式，首先構建一個人類可理解的概念節點圖，包括由多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）自動發現和驗證的新型高階概念，然後在這個概念圖上應用層次注意力來推斷主張的真實性。這種設計產生了可解釋的推理鏈，將證據與結論聯繫起來。實驗表明，PCGR在MMD準確性和對新興操控類型的穩健性方面達到了最先進的水平，在粗略檢測和細粒度操控識別方面均超越了先前的方法。
2603.25203.author: Ruichao Yang et.al.
2603.25203.authors: Ruichao Yang, Wei Gao, Xiaobin Zhu, Jing Ma, Hongzhan Lin, Ziyang Luo, Bo-Wen Zhang, Xu-Cheng Yin
